refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety constraints) to enable proper allocation and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Measure actual material moved (m³ or ton) and calculate hourly rate. Cross-check against baseline and safety incident log. Verify no safety plan violations occurred during measurement window.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target cycle time.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop) that do not reduce productivity below target when operated within policy.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry (material moved, cycle count, idle time) for performance monitoring.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-power hydraulic drivetrain and bucket control with low-latency operator feedback loop (local or remote) to minimize cycle time; enforce safety interlocks via real-time monitoring.

DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle performance (engine power, hydraulic flow, bucket capacity) and operator skill/interface (control latency, visibility, automation aids); optimize each independently.

DP-C: Implement closed-loop productivity control: measure material moved in real-time, compare to target, adjust vehicle parameters (engine RPM, bucket fill strategy) and operator guidance (task sequencing, speed recommendations) to maintain target rate.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle performance (engine power, hydraulic flow, bucket capacity) and operator skill/interface (control latency, visibility, automation aids); optimize each independently, with safety constraints enforced as hard limits.

potential_sources: Vehicle performance and operator interface are coupled via control latency and feedback bandwidth; however, this coupling is well-understood and can be managed via interface specification (e.g., max latency ≤ 200 ms for remote operation).
Safety constraints (geofence, speed limit) may couple with productivity if overly conservative; mitigation is to tune safety parameters based on worksite risk assessment, not blanket limits.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B is preferred over DP-A (monolithic) because it enables independent optimization of vehicle and operator subsystems and supports both local and remote operation modes. DP-C (closed-loop control) is deferred to lower decomposition levels as it requires sub-system maturity.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill and training are held constant or improved; risk: untrained operators may not achieve target even with optimized vehicle and interface.
Assumption: Worksite conditions (soil type, slope, material density) are representative of design basis; risk: off-design conditions (e.g., rocky terrain) may reduce productivity below target.
Assumption: Safety constraints can be tuned to allow target productivity; risk: if safety policy mandates very low speed or frequent stops, target may be unachievable.
Risk: Teleoperation latency and bandwidth may limit operator responsiveness; mitigation: specify max latency and min bandwidth in DP refinement.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly separate vehicle performance DP from operator interface DP, and add a third DP for safety constraint tuning. This will enable clearer allocation to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to deliver productivity KPI. It owns the end-to-end productivity measurement, safety compliance verification, and operator mode selection (local vs. remote).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits real-time productivity telemetry (material moved, cycle count, idle time, vehicle state) to Site Manager and Maintenance systems for monitoring and diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task assignments (dig location, load target, dump location) from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (geofence breach, emergency stop, safety constraint violation) to Safety Manager and Operator via SafetyAlert interface.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with remote Tele-operator (if remote mode) and to receive safety policy updates from Safety Manager.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for productivity KPI. It owns the integration logic, measurement, and safety compliance. Minimal diffusion: only the actual vehicle control and teleoperation execution are delegated to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

Vehicle module owns hydraulic drivetrain and bucket control, which are necessary but not sufficient for productivity KPI. Vehicle alone cannot measure productivity or enforce safety constraints; these require NGHE Solution integration.

Teleoperation module owns remote operator interface and control latency, which are necessary for remote operation but not sufficient for productivity KPI. Teleoperation alone cannot measure productivity or enforce safety constraints.

Safety Services module owns safety constraint enforcement (geofence, speed limit, emergency stop), which is necessary for safe productivity but not sufficient. Safety Services alone cannot measure productivity or optimize vehicle/operator performance.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 (Productivity KPI) is best owned by NGHE Solution because it requires integration of vehicle performance, operator interface, and safety constraints. The NGHE Solution module will own the productivity measurement logic, the decision logic for operator mode (local vs. remote), and the verification that safety constraints are met during productivity measurement.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if it owns too much integration logic; mitigation: clearly define interfaces between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and keep NGHE Solution focused on orchestration and measurement.

Risk: Productivity measurement may require real-time data from Vehicle (cycle count, material moved) and Safety Services (constraint violations); if these modules do not expose the required telemetry, NGHE Solution cannot measure productivity; mitigation: specify Telemetry interface requirements early.

Risk: Safety constraint tuning (e.g., speed limit) may be owned by Safety Services, but NGHE Solution must verify that tuned constraints allow target productivity; this creates a feedback loop that must be managed; mitigation: establish a safety-productivity trade-off review process.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules will expose the required telemetry (material moved, cycle count, constraint violations) via the Telemetry interface. Define the Tasking interface to support both local and remote task assignment. Establish a safety-productivity trade-off review process to tune safety constraints without compromising target productivity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-001 and sub-FRs clearly map to DP-B (decompose productivity into vehicle performance and operator interface). The DP is logically sound and addresses the key drivers of productivity (cycle time, operator responsiveness, safety constraints). No circular dependencies or missing mappings detected.

diffusion_risks: DP-B requires coordination between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules; if interfaces are not well-defined, implementation may diffuse across modules (e.g., productivity measurement logic scattered across multiple modules).

Teleoperation latency and bandwidth are critical to operator responsiveness; if Teleoperation module does not meet latency spec, Vehicle and Safety Services modules may not be able to compensate, leading to productivity shortfall.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module is the appropriate container for DP-B because it owns the integration logic and measurement. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules will implement their respective sub-DPs (vehicle performance, operator interface, safety constraints) and expose telemetry via well-defined interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trial. Productivity can be measured directly (material moved per hour). Safety compliance can be verified by cross-checking against safety incident log and constraint violation telemetry. Operator mode (local vs. remote) can be logged and analyzed.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) will be allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution in the next decomposition level.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-B to explicitly separate vehicle performance DP, operator interface DP, and safety constraint tuning DP. This will enable clearer allocation to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
Define the Telemetry interface specification (data types, update frequency, latency) to ensure NGHE Solution can measure productivity in real-time.
Establish a safety-productivity trade-off review process to tune safety constraints without compromising target productivity.
Specify the Tasking interface to support both local and remote task assignment, and define the operator mode selection logic in NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that has been successfully refined into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) and allocated to NGHE Solution. The FR-DP-IM mapping is sound and passes all gates. However, the sub-FRs are not yet atomic and require further decomposition to enable detailed design and implementation. Specifically, FR-NGHE-001.1 (vehicle cycle time) and FR-NGHE-001.2 (operator interface) require decomposition into lower-level FRs that can be allocated to specific Vehicle and Teleoperation sub-modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1.1: The vehicle hydraulic drivetrain shall deliver ≥ min_power to the bucket and drive motors to achieve cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target.
FR-NGHE-001.1.2: The vehicle bucket control system shall enable repeatable fill and dump cycles with ≤ fill_time_target and ≤ dump_time_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2.1: The operator interface (local or remote) shall provide real-time visual feedback (camera, instrument panel) with latency ≤ max_latency_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2.2: The operator control system (joystick, pedals, or remote input) shall respond to operator input with latency ≤ max_control_latency_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3.1: The safety system shall enforce geofence constraints without reducing vehicle speed below min_speed_for_productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3.2: The safety system shall enforce emergency stop with ≤ emergency_stop_distance without compromising operator safety.
FR-NGHE-001.4.1: The telemetry system shall measure and report material moved (m³ or ton) with accuracy ≥ measurement_accuracy_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4.2: The telemetry system shall measure and report cycle count and idle time with update frequency ≥ telemetry_update_frequency_target.

assumptions: Operator skill and training are held constant or improved during evaluation period.
Worksite conditions (soil type, slope, material density) are representative of design basis.
Safety constraints can be tuned to allow target productivity without compromising safety.
Teleoperation latency and bandwidth can be specified and met by Teleoperation module.
Vehicle hydraulic drivetrain and bucket control can be optimized to achieve target cycle time.
Productivity measurement can be automated via telemetry (material moved, cycle count) without manual intervention.
open_risks: Safety-productivity trade-off: if safety policy mandates very low speed or frequent stops, target productivity may be unachievable; mitigation: establish safety-productivity review process early.
Teleoperation latency: if remote operation latency exceeds operator tolerance, remote mode may not be viable; mitigation: specify max latency and conduct human factors testing.
Operator skill variability: untrained operators may not achieve target even with optimized vehicle and interface; mitigation: establish operator training and certification program.

Off-design worksite conditions: if actual worksite conditions differ significantly from design basis, productivity may fall short; mitigation: conduct site survey and adjust target or design accordingly.
Measurement accuracy: if productivity measurement is inaccurate or biased, evaluation results may be invalid; mitigation: validate measurement methodology and cross-check with manual observation.
evidence_needed: Baseline productivity data from current equipment or competitor benchmarks to validate target feasibility.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies to inform safety constraint tuning.
Operator skill assessment and training plan to ensure consistent performance.
Teleoperation latency and bandwidth specifications from Teleoperation module design.
Vehicle hydraulic drivetrain and bucket control performance data to validate cycle time target.
Productivity measurement methodology and validation plan to ensure accuracy and repeatability.
